summary: End-to-end testing of Foundation Apparel & Skin Labs e-commerce site. Backend: 9/9 pytest pass (after one minor fix). Frontend: all critical flows verified — home/shop/product/cart drawer/Stripe redirect/skin-labs/about/cart/404/mobile menu/newsletter. Stripe checkout session creation confirmed (URL contains checkout.stripe.com), payment_transactions row persisted with shipping=$6.99, server-side re-pricing verified.

minor.issue: Original code only handled 404 from Printify. Printify returns 422 for malformed product IDs which caused unhandled HTTPStatusError → 500 to client. Tests for non_existent_id and invalid product_id were failing.
minor.resolution: Fixed in /app/backend/server.py fetch_printify_product() — now treats 404/422/400 from Printify as 'not found' (returns None), letting upstream endpoints return proper 404 / 400. All 9 tests pass after fix.

minor.issue: When called with a bogus session_id (e.g. on /checkout/success?session_id=fake), Stripe SDK raises and FastAPI returns 500. Frontend console shows 'Failed to load resource: 500'. Frontend polls 12x so user eventually sees PAYMENT NOT COMPLETED, but server log noise + 500 status is not ideal. Recommend catching exception and returning 404/410 or a structured {status:'not_found'}.

issues: React Router v7 future-flag warnings in console (cosmetic only — recommend enabling v7_startTransition + v7_relativeSplatPath flags).

verified_flows.home: hero copy 'The foundation of your daily ritual' present; 4 featured product cards; hero-shop-btn and cart icon visible
verified_flows.shop: lists 4 products; search 'tee' filters to 2; sort dropdown present
verified_flows.product_detail: 6 thumbnails, 33 option chips (size/colour), add-to-cart enabled by default (auto-selects first enabled variant)
verified_flows.cart_drawer: opens on add-to-bag, cart-count badge shows 1, qty-increment works
verified_flows.payment_transactions_collection: Row inserted with status=initiated, payment_status=unpaid, shipping=6.99
verified_flows.newsletter: First call ok:true; second call ok:true, already_subscribed:true (idempotent)
verified_flows.skin_labs / about / cart / 404 pages: All routes render correctly via React Router
verified_flows.mobile_menu: Hamburger button opens menu, nav links visible at 390x844

action_items: Wrap Stripe SDK call in /api/checkout/status/{session_id} with try/except so an invalid/fake session_id returns a clean 404 instead of 500 (eliminates console noise on CheckoutSuccess fake-id flow).
Optional: enable React Router v7 future flags (v7_startTransition, v7_relativeSplatPath) to silence console warnings.
Optional: emergentintegrations StripeCheckout is instantiated on every request; consider memoising a single instance.
critical_code_review_comments: fetch_printify_product previously only mapped 404 to None — fixed to also cover 422/400.
checkout/status endpoint does not gracefully handle Stripe SDK exceptions for bogus session_ids.
CORS allow_origins='*' with allow_credentials=True is invalid per CORS spec; browsers ignore credentials in this combo. Tighten origins for production.
All interactive elements properly carry data-testid — testability is excellent.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (new pytest suite, 9 tests, JUnit XML output)
test_credentials: Not applicable — guest-checkout site, no auth.
seed_data_creation: None. Tests reuse the 4 live Printify products. Newsletter test inserts then cleans up its own row.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and pass 9/9. Stripe is in TEST mode — do not complete payment. CheckoutSuccess with fake session_id still emits 500 from /api/checkout/status — main agent should harden that endpoint with try/except. All frontend pages use proper data-testid selectors.
